description: Client-side routing — hash or history mode

createRouter.returns: { navigate: Function, outlet: Function, current: Function, path: Function, destroy: Function, onNavigate: Function }

navigate.description: Navigate programmatically. Delegates to active router.

params.name: props
params.type: { href: string, activeClass?: string, exact?: boolean, class?: string }

link.description: Create a router-aware anchor element with active link detection.

useRoute.returns: () => { path: string, params: Object, query: Object, component: Function|null, components: Function[], matched: boolean, name?: string, meta: Object }
useRoute.description: Get current route signal. The returned signal includes a `meta` field — an object merged from parent routes down to the matched leaf. Parent meta is applied first, child meta overrides. Example: `{ path: '/admin', meta: { requiresAuth: true, breadcrumb: 'Admin' }, component: AdminPage }`

useSearchParams.description: Reactive search params. Returns [getter, setter] tuple. Getter returns URLSearchParams from current URL query string. Setter updates query params without triggering full navigation.

onNavigate.description: Subscribe to navigation events on the active router. Returns an unsubscribe function.

back.description: Navigate back in history. Delegates to active router. /

forward.description: Navigate forward in history. Delegates to active router. /

isNavigating.description: Reactive boolean signal — true while lazy routes are resolving.
